findings.evidence: `rg -n "swapSides|toGridRectangle|interior|isEmpty" code/TauCeti/KnotTheory/Grid/RectangleCount.lean` shows `@[expose] def swapSides` at line 106 and only `swapSides_left`, `swapSides_right`, `swapSides_swapSides`, `swapSides_ne_self`; existing transformations in `Rectangle.lean` expose `*_toGridRectangle`, `interior_*`, and preservation lemmas instead of requiring unfolding.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from `swapSides` and add the missing characteristic lemmas, at least the interaction with `toGridRectangle`/`bottom`/`top` and any needed `interior`/emptiness/marking-avoidance restatements, following the existing `symm`/`transpose` API style.
findings.issue: `GridRectangleBetween.swapSides` is marked `@[expose]`, but the public API only gives side projections; consumers who need the promised complementary rectangle behavior must unfold the constructor body.

summary: The new count lemmas are broadly usable, but the public `swapSides` surface is exposed without the characteristic API needed to avoid unfolding it.
